wedding.en: a ceremony at which two people get married

ex.en: They invited over a hundred guests to their wedding.
ex.ko: 그들은 결혼식에 백 명이 넘는 손님을 초대했다.

weekday.en: any day of the week except Saturday and Sunday

ex.en: I go to work every weekday.
We have a weekly meeting on Mondays.
The magazine is published weekly.

weekly.en: a magazine or newspaper that is published every week

ex.en: She writes for a popular weekly.
ex.ko: 그녀는 인기 있는 주간지에 글을 쓴다.

weep.en: cry, especially because you are sad

ex.en: She wept quietly at the funeral.
This bag weighs about ten kilograms.
The nurse weighed the baby every week.

weigh.en: think carefully about something before making a decision

ex.en: She weighed the pros and cons carefully before deciding.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결정하기 전에 장단점을 신중히 따져보았다.

weigh.en: make someone feel worried or troubled

ex.en: The decision weighed heavily on his mind.
ex.ko: 그 결정은 그의 마음을 무겁게 짓눌렀다.

weight.en: how heavy a person or thing is

ex.en: He has lost a lot of weight recently.

weight.en: a heavy object lifted as exercise

ex.en: He lifts weights at the gym three times a week.
ex.ko: 그는 헬스장에서 일주일에 세 번 웨이트를 든다.

ex.en: Her opinion carries a lot of weight in this company.
ex.ko: 그녀의 의견은 이 회사에서 큰 비중을 차지한다.

well-known.en: known by a lot of people

ex.en: He is a well-known actor in his country.
ex.ko: 그는 자기 나라에서 잘 알려진 배우이다.

west.en: the direction you face to see the sun set

ex.en: The sun sets in the west.
They live on the west side of the city.
The road runs west from here.
ex.ko: 그 길은 여기서부터 서쪽으로 뻗어 있다.

west.en: the countries of Europe and North America, seen as a group

ex.en: The idea spread quickly from the West to Asia.
ex.ko: 그 사상은 서양에서 아시아로 빠르게 퍼졌다.

wet.en: covered or soaked with water or another liquid

ex.en: My shoes are still wet from the rain.
ex.ko: 내 신발은 비 때문에 아직 젖어 있다.

ex.en: Wet the cloth before you clean the window.

whatever.en: any thing or amount, no matter what

ex.en: You can choose whatever you like from the menu.
ex.ko: 메뉴에서 무엇이든 원하는 것을 고를 수 있어.

wheelchair.en: a chair with wheels used by people who cannot walk

ex.en: He has used a wheelchair since the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 사고 이후로 휠체어를 사용해 왔다.

while.en: during the time that something else is happening

ex.en: She called while I was cooking dinner.
ex.ko: 내가 저녁을 요리하는 동안 그녀가 전화했다.

ex.en: Let's stay here for a while.
While I like tea, my sister prefers coffee.
ex.ko: 나는 차를 좋아하는 반면 내 여동생은 커피를 더 좋아한다.

whole.en: complete, with no part left out

ex.en: She ate the whole cake by herself.
ex.ko: 그녀는 케이크를 통째로 혼자 다 먹었다.

whole.en: all of something; the entire amount

ex.en: The whole of the city was affected by the storm.

whom.en: used instead of 'who' as the object of a verb or preposition

ex.en: To whom should I address this letter?
ex.ko: 이 편지를 누구 앞으로 보내야 하나요?

wide.en: measuring a large distance from one side to the other

ex.en: The river is very wide here.

wide.en: as far or as much as possible

ex.en: She opened the door wide.

widely.en: over a large area, or among many people

ex.en: This tea is widely grown in Asia.
ex.ko: 이 차는 아시아 전역에서 널리 재배된다.

wild.en: living or growing in natural conditions, not controlled by people

ex.en: Wild flowers grew along the path.
The crowd went wild when the band appeared.

wild.en: a natural area or condition not affected by people

ex.en: These animals live in the wild.
It was too windy to go sailing.

wine.en: an alcoholic drink made from grapes

ex.en: They served red wine with dinner.
ex.ko: 그들은 저녁 식사에 레드 와인을 대접했다.

winner.en: the person who wins a competition or game

ex.en: The winner received a gold medal.

winner.en: someone or something that is very successful

ex.en: This new recipe is a real winner with the kids.
ex.ko: 이 새 레시피는 아이들에게 정말 인기다.

wisdom.en: the ability to use knowledge and experience to make good decisions

ex.en: The elderly often share wisdom with younger generations.
ex.ko: 노인들은 흔히 젊은 세대에게 지혜를 나눠준다.

wise.en: showing good judgment based on knowledge and experience

ex.en: It was wise of you to save some money.
ex.ko: 돈을 좀 저축해둔 건 현명한 일이었다.

within.en: inside a particular period of time, distance, or amount

ex.en: The package will arrive within three days.

without.en: not having, using, or accompanied by something

ex.en: I can't live without my phone.
ex.ko: 나는 휴대폰 없이는 살 수 없다.

wonder.en: want to know something and think about it

ex.en: I wonder what she is doing now.
ex.ko: 그녀가 지금 뭘 하고 있는지 궁금하다.

wonder.en: a feeling of surprise and admiration

ex.en: The children looked at the fireworks in wonder.

wood.en: the hard material that trees are made of

ex.en: The table is made of wood.

wood.en: an area of land covered with trees

ex.en: They went for a walk in the woods.
She sat on an old wooden chair.

wool.en: the soft thick hair of sheep, or cloth made from it

ex.en: This sweater is made of pure wool.

worldwide.en: happening or existing in all parts of the world

ex.en: The company has offices worldwide.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 전 세계에 사무소를 두고 있다.

worldwide.en: in all parts of the world

ex.en: The film became popular worldwide.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 전 세계적으로 인기를 얻었다.

worried.en: feeling anxious about a problem or something that might happen

ex.en: She looked worried about the exam results.

worse.en: more bad; the comparative form of 'bad'

ex.en: The traffic today is worse than yesterday.
ex.ko: 오늘 교통 체증이 어제보다 더 심하다.

worse.en: in a more bad or serious way; the comparative form of 'badly'

ex.en: He plays worse than his brother.

worst.en: most bad; the superlative form of 'bad'

ex.en: This is the worst movie I've ever seen.
ex.ko: 이건 내가 본 영화 중 최악이다.

worst.en: the most bad situation or thing

ex.en: Prepare for the worst.

worst.en: in the most bad way; the superlative form of 'badly'

ex.en: Of all of us, she was affected worst by the news.
ex.ko: 우리 중에서 그녀가 그 소식에 가장 나쁜 영향을 받았다.

writing.en: the activity of writing, or something that has been written

ex.en: Her writing has improved a lot this year.
ex.ko: 그녀의 글쓰기 실력이 올해 많이 늘었다.

ex.en: "Are you coming?" "Yeah, sure."

youth.en: the period of someone's life when they are young

ex.en: He spent his youth in the countryside.

youth.en: a young person, or young people in general

ex.en: Many youths gathered at the festival.

zone.en: an area with a particular purpose or feature

ex.en: This is a no-parking zone.
